strict_mode_undeclared.root_cause: QuickJS (rquickjs 0.8.1) evaluates scripts in strict mode by default — undeclared variable assignments (e.g. `url = ...` without `var`/`let`/`const`) throw ReferenceError instead of creating a global. Legado's Rhino runs in non-strict mode where this creates a global. IIFE wrapping does NOT help because strictness is inherited.
strict_mode_undeclared.reader_source: crates/reader-js/src/lib.rs:924 (ctx.eval) — JS_EVAL_STRICT flag is applied by rquickjs default EvalOptions
strict_mode_undeclared.classification: 完全未实现 (engine-level strict mode mismatch)
strict_mode_undeclared.fix_recommendation: Either (a) patch rquickjs to clear JS_EVAL_STRICT flag for user-JS eval, OR (b) replace QuickJS with an engine that supports non-strict mode (e.g. V8 with per-context strict=false), OR (c) pre-process user JS to insert `var` declarations for all free variables (fragile). IIFE wrapping confirmed NOT to work (test 4 in strict-mode repro).
strict_mode_undeclared.severity: P0 — blocks 15 sources (28% of js_unsupported failures)
strict_mode_undeclared.evidence_repro: Confirmed via standalone rquickjs test: `ctx.eval("url = 'hello'; url")` → 'url is not defined'; even `(function(){ url = 'inner'; return url; })()` → same error. `with({a:1}){a}` → 'invalid keyword: with'.

source_object_method_missing.root_cause: Reader injects `source` as `{}` (empty object) per crates/reader-content/src/lib.rs:1419-1423. Legado's BookSource model exposes `getKey()`, `header`, `loginUrl`, `getVariable()` methods/fields. Sources call `source.getKey()`, `source.header`, `source.key`, `source.loginUrl` and get 'not a function' / undefined.
source_object_method_missing.legado_source: app/src/main/java/io/legado/app/data/entities/BookSource.kt — getKey() returns bookSourceUrl; header is a JSON string field; loginUrl is a String field; getVariable() reads source-level variable
source_object_method_missing.reader_source: crates/reader-content/src/lib.rs:1419-1423 (source defaults to `{}` when caller doesn't provide); reader-cli test_source.rs never populates source either
source_object_method_missing.classification: 已实现但调用方式不同 (source object shape mismatch)
source_object_method_missing.fix_recommendation: Inject `source` as a JSON object literal with: `bookSourceUrl` (=source URL), `key` (alias for bookSourceUrl), `header` (source.headers JSON string), `loginUrl` (source.loginUrl), `bookSourceName`, plus a `getVariable()` method (or `variables` map). Method-style calls like `source.getKey()` require a JS proxy wrapper that exposes methods. Add a host callback `java.getSource` returning the full source object (descriptor already exists in lib.rs:218).
source_object_method_missing.severity: P0 — blocks 13 sources (24% of js_unsupported failures)

rule_engine_json_parse.root_cause: JS evaluation returns a non-JSON-serializable value (undefined, function, or empty) and Reader's rule engine then fails to parse it as JSON. Detail: 'invalid JSON input: expected value at line 1 column 1'.
rule_engine_json_parse.legado_source: app/src/main/java/io/legado/app/model/analyzeRule/AnalyzeRule.kt — Rhino's toString() coerces undefined to 'undefined' string; Legado's rule engine tolerates non-JSON strings
rule_engine_json_parse.reader_source: crates/reader-js/src/lib.rs:928 (quickjs_value_to_json) — undefined is not a JSON value; serde_json::to_string(undefined) fails
rule_engine_json_parse.classification: 完全未实现 (rule engine assumes JSON, JS can return undefined)
rule_engine_json_parse.fix_recommendation: When JS evaluates to undefined/null, coerce to empty string `""` before JSON parse. Mirror Legado's Rhino string-coercion semantics: `String(result)` always returns a string. Update `evaluate_url_js` (crates/reader-content/src/lib.rs:1469-1472) and `quickjs_value_to_json` to coerce undefined → empty string.
rule_engine_json_parse.severity: P1 — blocks 11 sources (20% of js_unsupported failures)

java_put_signature_mismatch.root_cause: Reader's `java.put` requires both key and value to be strings (ResidualArgShape::KeyValueStrings in lib.rs:7708). Legado's `java.put(key: String, value: Any)` accepts any value and stores it via `chapter.putVariable(key, value.toString())`. Sources call `java.put("extra", true)`, `java.put("page", page)` (page is a number), etc.
java_put_signature_mismatch.legado_source: app/src/main/java/io/legado/app/model/analyzeRule/AnalyzeUrl.kt:373 — `fun put(key: String, value: String)` — but Rhino auto-coerces non-strings to strings via toString()
java_put_signature_mismatch.reader_source: crates/reader-js/src/lib.rs:7708 (make_residual_host_callback for java.put with KeyValueStrings shape)
java_put_signature_mismatch.classification: 已实现但调用方式不同 (signature mismatch — Reader requires strings, Legado auto-coerces)
java_put_signature_mismatch.fix_recommendation: Loosen `java.put` value argument: accept any JSON value (string, number, boolean, object) and coerce to string before storing. Keep key as string. This mirrors Legado's Rhino auto-coercion. Update ResidualArgShape::KeyValueStrings validation to allow non-string values.
java_put_signature_mismatch.severity: P1 — blocks 5 sources (9% of js_unsupported failures)

source_object_field_missing.root_cause: Sources call `java.ajax(source.key)` or `java.ajax(source.getKey())` expecting `source.key` to return the source's base URL. Reader's `source` literal is `{}` so `source.key` is undefined → `java.ajax` rejects non-string URL.
source_object_field_missing.classification: 已实现但调用方式不同 (source object missing key field)
source_object_field_missing.fix_recommendation: Same fix as `source_object_method_missing` — populate `source.key` (= bookSourceUrl) and `source.bookSourceUrl` in the injected source literal.
source_object_field_missing.severity: P1 — blocks 3 sources (6% of js_unsupported failures)

host_callback_unregistered.root_cause: `java.androidId` is registered in Core as a host callback descriptor (lib.rs:7912), but the CLI test host (tools/reader-cli/src/test_source.rs) does NOT register a handler for it. When the JS calls `java.androidId()`, the host callback dispatch fails with 'unregistered host callback: java.androidId'.
host_callback_unregistered.classification: host callback 路由已实现但运行时未接通 (CLI test host gap)
host_callback_unregistered.fix_recommendation: Register a stub host callback for `java.androidId` in the CLI test host (e.g. return a fixed test device ID like 'reader-cli-test-device-id'). Real platform hosts (iOS/Android/HarmonyOS) will register the real device ID. This is a CLI tool gap, not a Core gap.

with_statement_forbidden.root_cause: Source uses Rhino-specific `JavaImporter` + `Packages.java.lang` + `with(javaImport){...}` to import Java crypto classes for AES encryption. QuickJS forbids `with` statement (strict mode) and has no Java interop. This is fundamentally incompatible with non-Rhino engines.
with_statement_forbidden.legado_source: modules/rhino/src/main/java/com/script/rhino/RhinoScriptEngine.kt — Rhino provides full Java interop via Packages/JavaImporter
with_statement_forbidden.reader_source: crates/reader-js/src/lib.rs:924 — QuickJS has no Java interop
with_statement_forbidden.classification: 完全未实现 (engine-level: QuickJS has no Java interop, `with` forbidden)
with_statement_forbidden.fix_recommendation: Cannot be fixed at engine level. Options: (a) port the source's JS to pure ES (use reader-js's `java.createSymmetricCrypto`/`java.aesEncodeToBase64String` instead of javax.crypto), (b) document as 'incompatible source — requires Rhino', (c) long-term: embed a JS engine with Java interop on Android only (keeping QuickJS for iOS/HarmonyOS). This is a known Legado ecosystem dependency on Rhino's Java interop.

nan_non_json.root_cause: Source JS produces NaN (e.g. `1100000000+parseInt(result)` where `result` is non-numeric → NaN). Reader's quickjs_value_to_json rejects non-finite numbers. Legado's Rhino serializes NaN as `null` or coerces to string 'NaN'.
nan_non_json.classification: 完全未实现 (JSON serialization of NaN/Infinity)
nan_non_json.fix_recommendation: In `quickjs_value_to_json`, coerce NaN/Infinity to `null` (matching standard JSON.stringify behavior, which Legado follows). This is a 1-line fix in the value-to-JSON converter.

host_callback_redline.root_cause: Source calls `java.webView(null, baseUrl, null)`. Core intentionally refuses with 'java.webView is unsupported in Core (red line 4: Core does not touch WebView) — host platform adapter must handle'. CLI test host has no WebView. This is by design (Core/Host boundary).
host_callback_redline.reader_source: crates/reader-js/src/lib.rs:7724 (descriptor registered, dispatch rejected by Core red-line check)
host_callback_redline.classification: host callback 路由已实现但运行时未接通 (intentional — CLI has no WebView)
host_callback_redline.fix_recommendation: This is correct behavior per red line 4. The CLI test host should register a stub that returns null (or a clear error), and the failure should be classified as 'host_webview_unsupported' rather than 'js_unsupported'. Real platform hosts (iOS WKWebView, Android WebView, HarmonyOS WebView) will register real handlers. No Core change needed.
host_callback_redline.severity: P3 — blocks 1 source (intentional, requires platform WebView)

url_dsl_malformed.root_cause: Source-side malformed URL DSL: `{"body": "id"="search-form"}` is invalid JSON/JS (the `=` should be `:`). Reader's UrlDslParser rejects this. Legado's parser is more lenient (or the source is also broken in Legado).
url_dsl_malformed.fix_recommendation: No fix needed. This is a source-side bug. Optionally, add a 'url_dsl_malformed' failure reason distinct from 'js_unsupported' for clearer reporting.

host_callback_network.root_cause: Source calls `java.ajax(url)` which makes a real HTTP request via CLI's ureq client. The request timed out. This is a network timeout, NOT a JS unsupported method. Misclassified as js_unsupported because the failure originated in a JS-initiated host callback.
host_callback_network.classification: 分类错误 (misclassification — should be http_error, not js_unsupported)
host_callback_network.fix_recommendation: In test_source.rs error classification, distinguish between JS engine errors (js_unsupported) and host callback network errors (http_error). A `java.ajax` network timeout should be classified as http_error.
host_callback_network.severity: P2 — blocks 1 source (misclassification, not a real JS gap)

jsoup_global_not_exposed.root_cause: Source calls `org.jsoup.Jsoup.parse(body).select(...)` expecting Jsoup HTML parser as a JS global. Legado exposes Jsoup via Rhino's Java interop (`Packages.org.jsoup.Jsoup`). Reader has no Jsoup global in JS.
jsoup_global_not_exposed.legado_source: app/src/main/java/io/legado/app/help/JsExtensions.kt:57 — `import org.jsoup.Jsoup`; Rhino exposes all Java classes via Packages
jsoup_global_not_exposed.classification: 完全未实现 (Jsoup global not exposed in JS sandbox)
jsoup_global_not_exposed.fix_recommendation: Register a `Jsoup` global object in the JS sandbox backed by Reader's existing HTML parser (reader-rule crate uses `scraper` which is a Jsoup-equivalent). Expose `Jsoup.parse(html)` returning a node with `.select(cssSelector)` returning an array-like. This is a moderate effort but unblocks Jsoup-dependent sources. Alternatively, document a workaround: use Reader's `java.getString(ruleStr)` rule-engine API instead of direct Jsoup access.
jsoup_global_not_exposed.severity: P2 — blocks 1 source (but likely affects more sources not in this batch)

results.result: Err: 'url is not defined' (explicit strict, same)

strict_mode_repro.conclusion: QuickJS evaluates scripts in strict mode by default via rquickjs. IIFE wrapping does NOT help because strictness is inherited by nested function bodies. Only fix is to clear JS_EVAL_STRICT flag at the FFI level or use a different engine.

legado_prelude_bindings.description: Legado AnalyzeUrl.kt:348-361 evalJS bindings vs Reader evaluate_url_js
legado_prelude_bindings.note: Missing `cache` affects src-001 (起点读书: cache.get('fx')). Missing `book` does not affect any v6 failure but may affect detail/toc/content stages.

legado_js_methods_audit.description: Legado JsExtensions.kt method count vs reader-js registered java.* methods
legado_js_methods_audit.reader_registered_count: 79 (all Legado methods have descriptors in lib.rs HostDescriptor enum)
legado_js_methods_audit.note: CLI test host only wires HTTP-capable callbacks. Non-HTTP callbacks (androidId, webView, file ops, font, etc.) fail with 'unregistered host callback' when called. This is a CLI test host gap, not a Core gap. Real platform hosts must wire all 37 non-HTTP callbacks.

fix_priority_order.fix: Patch rquickjs to clear JS_EVAL_STRICT flag for user-JS eval (blocks 15 sources)
Populate `source` JS object with bookSourceUrl/key/header/loginUrl fields + getKey()/getVariable() methods (blocks 13+3=16 sources)
Coerce undefined/NaN JS results to empty string/null in rule engine (blocks 11+1=12 sources)
Loosen java.put value argument to accept any JSON value, coerce to string (blocks 5 sources)
Register stub host callbacks for java.androidId and other non-HTTP callbacks in CLI test host (blocks 1 source, unblocks real platform testing)
Reclassify java.ajax network timeouts as http_error, not js_unsupported (blocks 1 source misclassification)
Expose Jsoup global in JS sandbox backed by reader-rule's scraper (blocks 1 source)
Document java.webView as 'host-only, requires platform WebView' (blocks 1 source, intentional)
Document Rhino JavaImporter+with sources as 'engine-incompatible, requires source JS port' (blocks 1 source)
Add 'url_dsl_malformed' failure reason distinct from js_unsupported (blocks 1 source, source-side bug)
